commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds gateway session-store alias helpers and routes session patch/delete/reset/writeback/resolve/compaction/checkpoint paths through raw-alias synchronization with regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main prunes raw external aliases during canonicalization, and the PR-specific remaining gap follows from listing one canonical row, then calling a lifecycle method with that canonical key and getting an empty preserved alias list.
commands.review_followup: A narrow PR-branch repair can address the blocking alias-discovery gap without a product decision.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve raw external session aliases
target.body:
Closes #63005.

## Behavior addressed

External raw session keys such as `conversation:pair:...` and opaque provider keys such as `Signal:Group:<mixed-case-id>` should survive gateway session-key canonicalization. A nested `sessions_send`, agent-scoped lookup, checkpoint restore, compaction checkpoint metadata write, or manual/maxLines compaction metadata update should resolve to the same stored session instead of splitting history across raw and `agent:<id>:` aliases.

## Root cause

`migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey` canonicalized raw external keys before preserving the raw-key store entry as an alias of the canonical agent key. The first fix preserved that raw alias, but several mutation paths still replaced, mutated, or deleted only the canonical `primaryKey`. That left the raw alias stale after replacement-style writes.

ClawSweeper caught the remaining lifecycle gaps: `sessions.compaction.restore`, `persistSessionCompactionCheckpoint`, and `sessions.compact` metadata updates could still write only the canonical entry, so raw and canonical aliases could diverge again after checkpoint/compaction flows. It also caught that preserved raw aliases must use the existing opaque-ID-preserving session-key normalization contract instead of lowercasing Signal group IDs.

## Fix

The migration helper now returns explicit `preservedAliasKeys`. Shared store helpers synchronize those aliases on writes and remove them on delete. Replacement/delete/metadata paths now use the shared helpers, including:

- `sessions.patch`
- `sessions.delete`
- `sessions.reset`
- agent writeback
- node-event store touches
- resolver sync
- `sessions.compaction.restore`
- compaction checkpoint metadata persistence
- `sessions.compact` manual compaction metadata updates
- `sessions.compact` maxLines metadata updates

Preserved raw aliases stay internal-only for lookup continuity: `sessions.list` filtering/search/count collapses a raw+canonical alias pair into one visible canonical session row.

Opaque Signal group peer IDs are preserved when deriving raw aliases, so a mixed-case group ID does not create a lowercased duplicate logical session row.

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Raw external session aliases are preserved during canonicalization, stay synchronized when canonical entries are replaced/deleted, stay synchronized across checkpoint restore, checkpoint metadata persistence, manual compaction metadata updates, and maxLines compaction metadata updates, preserve opaque Signal group peer-ID casing, and do not duplicate visible list/search/count rows.

**Real environment tested:** Source checkout on PR head `75b35bb408bda9559bcc31d8126ccfe3e4471d03`, rebased onto `upstream/main` `f07c87405c3094feceaaf2f0f19a2e765a25e78d`, using Node 22 with repo dependencies installed.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a direct `node --import tsx` source-checkout proof against `src/gateway/session-utils.ts` to migrate a raw key, write a canonical replacement, list/search visible rows, and delete the alias pair. Also ran the gateway checkpoint/compaction regression suite listed in `Validation`.

**Evidence after fix:** Direct terminal output from the rebased current head:

```json
{
  "primaryKey": "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123",
  "preservedAliasKeys": [
    "conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
  ],
  "afterWrite": {
    "canonicalSessionId": "sess-write",
    "rawSessionId": "sess-write",
    "rawSendPolicy": "deny",
    "sameEntry": true
  },
  "visible": {
    "listKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "listCount": 1,
    "listTotalCount": 1,
    "searchKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "searchCount": 1,
    "searchTotalCount": 1
  },
  "deleted": true,
  "afterDelete": {
    "canonicalSessionId": null,
    "rawSessionId": null
  }
}
```

Checkpoint/compaction-specific evidence from the rebased current-head tests:

- `src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts` proves `sessions.compaction.restore` updates both raw and canonical aliases and keeps only the canonical row visible.
- `src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts` proves checkpoint metadata persistence writes the checkpoint to both raw and canonical aliases.
- `src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts` proves manual `sessions.compact` metadata updates synchronize both aliases after embedded compaction.
- `src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts` proves `sessions.compact` with `maxLines` clears token metadata on both aliases and keeps only the canonical row visible.
- `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts` proves a mixed-case Signal group raw key preserves the opaque peer ID, synchronizes write/delete aliases, prunes the folded alias, and lists one visible canonical row.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw alias and canonical key share the same written entry after replacement, including the patched `sendPolicy`; list and search return one visible canonical row with `totalCount: 1`; delete removes both keys. The checkpoint restore, checkpoint metadata, manual compaction, and maxLines compaction regressions verify both raw and canonical keys receive the same lifecycle updates. The Signal regression verifies opaque peer-ID casing is preserved and the folded lowercased alias is removed.

**What was not tested:** No full live external chat-channel round trip was run. No screenshots are attached because this is backend session-store behavior with no visual UI surface.

## Validation

Ran on the rebased head:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       273 passed (273)
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       30 passed (30)
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts
Test Files  15 passed (15)
Tests       579 passed (579)
```

```text
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

```text
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.ts src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Also ran `pnpm docs:list` before editing. Broad Testbox changed gate was not run because the `blacksmith` CLI is not installed in this environment; I kept local validation to targeted gateway proof.
